assertions.expectation: Recommends Stable or Extended release channel to minimize upgrade frequency
assertions.reason: The response recommends Regular channel instead of Stable or Extended, stating that Regular provides better security patch velocity than Stable

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' maintenance exclusion to block disruptive upgrades while allowing security patches on the control plane
assertions.reason: The response explicitly shows using --add-maintenance-exclusion-scope no_minor_or_node_upgrades and explains this allows control plane security patches while blocking disruptive upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends a narrow maintenance window (e.g., weekends only) so CP patches happen at predictable times
assertions.reason: The response configures a Sunday 4-hour maintenance window (06:00-10:00 UTC weekly) and explains it should align with training schedule gaps

assertions.expectation: Suggests isolating training workloads on dedicated GPU node pools separate from serving/infrastructure pools
assertions.reason: The response explicitly creates separate node pools: training-h100 for GPU workloads and system-cpu for system/monitoring workloads, explaining the separation of concerns approach

assertions.expectation: Recommends disabling auto-upgrade on GPU training node pools and upgrading manually during scheduled gaps between training runs
assertions.reason: The response shows manual upgrade commands for the H100 node pool and explains using the cordon-and-wait pattern during planned upgrades between training campaigns

assertions.expectation: Advises configuring training frameworks with checkpointing so jobs can resume after any disruption
assertions.reason: The response includes a checkpointing strategy section with automatic checkpointing, SIGTERM handling, and resume from checkpoint capability requirements

assertions.expectation: Mentions GPU driver version testing — always validate in staging before applying to production GPU pools
assertions.reason: While the response mentions GPU driver compatibility is handled by GKE and includes driver version confirmation in a checklist, it does not specifically recommend testing in staging before production

assertions.expectation: Notes that GPUDirect/RDMA networking has strict GKE version requirements — verify compatibility before upgrading
assertions.reason: The response mentions compact placement preserves RDMA topology for H100 interconnect but does not discuss GKE version compatibility requirements for GPUDirect/RDMA networking
